# sysande

sysande is an open-source definition repository for AI-assisted system analysis and design.

It is not a new diagramming language, renderer, implementation framework, source-code generator, or AI service.

Normal sysande output is documentation and diagrams: discovery summaries, coverage plans, assumptions, decisions, source DSL files, generated diagrams, and review notes.

Start here:
- /AGENTS.md (for AI diagramming guidelines)
- /definitions/workflow.md
- /definitions/coverage.md
- /definitions/scope.md
- /definitions/c4-output.md
- /prompts/00-init.md

Core workflow:
1. Ask init questions iteratively.
2. Summarize the system, scope, constraints, assumptions, and open questions.
3. Create a coverage plan.
4. Ask the user to approve or revise the plan.
5. Design one coverage view at a time.
6. Use established DSLs and tools.
7. Reuse shared model elements and identifiers.
8. Review every view with the user.
9. Run a final cross-view consistency review.

Preferred tools:
- C4 / Structurizr for rapid structural architecture views that can be pasted into Structurizr Playground.
- C4 / LikeC4 for repo-native shared architecture models when that workflow is useful.
- D2 for data-flow, state-machine, sequence-style behavior, ERD, custom diagrams, open visual themes, and animated SVG.

Important rules:
- Do not generate every possible diagram.
- Do not generate diagrams before the initial coverage plan is reviewed.
- Do not generate implementation source code, package manifests, build config, or framework scaffolds.
- Do not choose a technology stack unless it is already a confirmed project constraint.
- Do not mix abstraction levels without a clear reason.
- Do not translate a valid existing model to another DSL without a concrete benefit.
- Treat generated SVG as an output, never as the source of truth.
- For C4 structural views, prefer Playground-compatible Structurizr DSL unless the user requests LikeC4 or an existing project already uses LikeC4.
